title: helianthemum thymifolium var. glutinosum
intro: Helianthemum thymifolium var. glutinosum, commonly known as sticky rockrose, is a plant species in the family Cistaceae. This plant is known to possess various health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable resource for traditional medicine. The therapeutic actions of Helianthemum thymifolium var. glutinosum include soothing and calming effects on the skin, which may help to alleviate issues such as eczema and dermatitis. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties and potential health benefits.

parts: Helianthemum thymifolium var. glutinosum has many medicinal parts, such as its flowers, leaves, and stems, which are traditionally used in folk medicine. The flowers of this plant are rich in flavonoids, particularly quercetin and kaempferol, which exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The leaves and stems of Helianthemum thymifolium var. glutinosum contain iridoid glycosides, including aucubin and catalpol, which have been shown to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities. The roots of this plant are not commonly mentioned as a medicinal part, however, some sources suggest they may contain terpenoids and phenolic acids, although more research is needed to confirm this.

side_effects: Helianthemum thymifolium var. glutinosum can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal problems, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, due to its high saponin content. Additionally, excessive consumption of the plant may lead to allergic reactions, including skin irritation, respiratory issues, and anaphylaxis in severe cases.
